methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ to date, including C$23M from the Quantum Champions initiative in December 2025. The valuation exercise examines a universe of 11 quantum computing comparables—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The quantum computing sector contains only 5 same-sector peers, but filtering by stage, adjacent development timing, or valuation tier yielded zero datapoints meeting the minimum threshold of three comparable firms. A sector-wide median across all quantum computing companies would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in business models, technical approaches, and funding stages within the space.

Without sufficient narrowly-matched peers, traditional comparable-company triangulation cannot be reliably executed. This analysis therefore cannot derive a supported valuation range using the data provided. Any valuation conclusion would require either: expansion of the comp set beyond quantum computing into adjacent deep-tech sectors, application of alternative methods (venture cost-of-capital, sum-of-the-parts), or acceptance of wider confidence intervals reflecting structural scarcity of comparable transactions at seed stage in nascent quantum infrastructure. The key uncertainty remains the absence of pricing benchmarks from quantum computing firms at Nord Quantique's stage and capital deployment profile.
